agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive 2025 Homeless Count report for Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a report and presentation on the 2025 Homeless Count for Vancouver. This is an information item only — no bylaw or regulation is being proposed. The report provides data on homelessness in the region, which may inform future policy decisions that could affect businesses, particularly those in the Hotel & Accommodation and Retail sectors. No speakers are allowed during this agenda item per the Procedure Bylaw.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	2025 Homeless Count – Vancouver
(no speakers per section 7.2(d) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
Presentation
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 Cultural Grants for Operating & Spaces
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed 2026 Cultural Grants that would provide funding for arts and culture organizations in Burnaby. The grants cover two areas: 'Operating' (money to help run a cultural group day-to-day) and 'Cultural Spaces' (money for buildings or venues used for arts). Some parts of this proposal need at least two-thirds of all council members to vote 'yes' to pass, as required by Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter (a special rule for certain funding decisions). This is not yet approved — council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you run a Burnaby arts or culture group, watch for the council decision — this could affect your 2026 funding.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	2026 Cultural Grants (Balance of Operating, Cultural Spaces)
Recommendations A to C, E and G require at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Community Sport Event Grants for Spring 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to award grants to community sport events happening in Vancouver during the Spring of 2026. This decision requires at least two-thirds of all council members to vote 'yes' — a special voting rule under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter (the city's main governing law). If approved, the grants would help local organizers cover costs for events that bring people together through sports.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you organize a community sport event in Vancouver, watch for the council vote — this could open a grant application window for Spring 2026.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	Vancouver Community Sport Event Grants – Spring 2026 Intake
Recommendation A requires at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby property tax distribution for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on how to divide the total property tax levy among different classes of properties (like homes, businesses, and industry) for the 2026 tax year. This proposed decision will determine how much of the overall tax burden falls on each type of property owner. A 'property tax levy' is the total amount of money the city needs to collect from property taxes. The proposed distribution would set the tax rates for each property class. This is not yet approved — it is up for discussion and a vote at the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your property tax rate for 2026.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.	2026 Property Taxation: Distribution of Property Tax Levy
4a. Budget Resolution
4b. Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 property tax exemptions for non-profits
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a report that recommends granting property tax exemptions for 2025 to certain non-profit organizations in Burnaby. A proposed bylaw would allow eligible charities, churches, and community groups to pay less or no property tax. This is not yet approved — council will debate and decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from most business owners. If you run a non-profit, watch for the council decision to see if your organization qualifies.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
5.	2025 Property Tax Exemptions
(no speakers per section 7.2(d) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Coastal Flood Management Policy
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed Coastal Flood Management Policy. This policy would create new rules for building and development in areas of Burnaby that are at risk of flooding from the ocean. If approved, property owners in these flood-risk zones may face new requirements for building elevations, flood-proofing, and insurance. The goal is to protect people and property from rising sea levels and storm surges. The report is up for discussion at the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Check if your property is in a proposed flood-risk zone and review the draft policy before the council vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Coastal Flood Management Policy — council discussion date

agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Insurance & Finance
agenda_briefs.source_body:
6.	Coastal Flood Management Policy
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Heritage Foundation Board Annual Report 2025
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a recommendation (Recommendation B) regarding the Vancouver Heritage Foundation Board's Annual Report for 2025. This recommendation requires at least two-thirds of all council members to vote in favour, as stated under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter. The Vancouver Charter is the city's main governing law. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.	Vancouver Heritage Foundation Board – Annual Report 2025
Recommendation B requires at least 2/3 affirmative votes of all Council members under Section 206(1) of theVancouver Charter
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on public plaza alcohol sites for 2026-2029
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow alcohol consumption in certain public plazas from 2026 to 2029. This is part of the 'Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program.' If approved, the program would let people drink alcohol in designated outdoor public spaces during specific hours and dates. The council will consider which plazas to include and what rules will apply. This is not yet law — it is a proposed decision that could change before a final vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect where your customers or staff can drink alcohol in public plazas.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.	Sites for 2026-2029 Alcohol Consumption in Public Plazas Program
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby seeks grant to speed up development approvals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider applying for a provincial grant to help speed up the city's development approval process. The proposed 'Local Government Development Approvals Program' grant would fund changes to make it faster and easier for builders and developers to get permits and approvals. This is not yet approved — council will vote on whether to submit the funding application.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for council's decision on whether to apply for this grant.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.	Funding Application to Local Government Development Approvals Program
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on housing rezoning rule changes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a package of miscellaneous amendments that would change the conditions attached to various housing rezoning approvals in Burnaby. A proposed bylaw would update, clarify, or remove certain requirements that developers must meet when they get a property rezoned for housing. This could affect what builders are required to include in new housing projects, such as community amenities, public benefits, or design standards. The exact changes are detailed in a staff report that council will consider at an upcoming meeting. No decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the staff report to see if your development project is affected.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
10.	Miscellaneous Amendments Concerning Various Housing Rezoning Conditions
(no speakers per section 7.2(b) of the Procedure By-law)
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Text amendment to CD-1 (839) bylaw for 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed text amendment to the Comprehensive Development (CD-1) Bylaw No. 13493 for two properties: 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue. A CD-1 bylaw is a special zoning rule that allows a specific development plan for a site. This amendment would change the written rules (the 'text') of that bylaw, not the map or boundaries. The exact changes are described in the draft bylaw included in the council agenda. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect development rules for these properties.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.	CD-1 (839) Text Amendment: 5590 Victoria Drive and 2005 East 40th Avenue
(no speakers per section 7.2(b) of the Procedure By-law)
Report

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning at 4088 Granville Street, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for 4088 Granville Street. If approved, the property would be rezoned to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) district, which is a special zoning category that allows for a specific development plan. This would change the current zoning rules for this property. The decision is part of a draft bylaw to amend the city's Zoning and Development By-law. The proposal is on the council agenda for discussion and a vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and development plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for 3803-3823 West 10th Ave & 2553 Highbury St
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for properties at 3803-3823 West 10th Avenue and 2553 Highbury Street. The plan would change the land-use rules (called a CD-1 rezoning) to allow a new development on these sites. The proposal includes draft bylaws to update the CD-1 bylaw, the Zoning and Development Bylaw, and the Sign Bylaw. This is not yet approved — council will consider and vote on the proposal at a future meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and public hearing notice — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and development plans.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning for 310 East 14th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 310 East 14th Avenue. This is a 'CD-1' rezoning, which means the land would be given a special 'Comprehensive Development' zone. This type of zone allows the city to set custom rules for what can be built on that specific property, instead of following the standard zoning rules. The draft bylaw would amend Burnaby's Zoning and Development Bylaw. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming item on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this rezoning could affect nearby property values or development plans.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning at 1305-1325 West 13th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1305-1325 West 13th Avenue. A 'rezoning' means changing the official rules about what can be built on that land. This is a first step — the draft bylaw is up for approval at the next council meeting. If passed, it would amend the city's Zoning and Development Bylaw. No public speakers are scheduled for this item under the council's procedure rules.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — monitor the council decision for changes to development rules on this site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to consult on new development cost charges
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a report on the 2026 Financing Growth Update. This update includes proposed changes to the Amenity Cost Charge Bylaw and updates to Density Bonus and Inclusionary Zoning rules under Bill 16. These are tools the city uses to charge developers fees for community amenities like parks and community centres. The report outlines required public consultation before any new bylaw is passed. A decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for public consultation dates — you may want to provide input on how new development fees could affect your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
16.	2026 Financing Growth Update – Required Consultation for Amenity Cost Charge By law and Bill 16 Density Bonus and Inclusionary Zoning Updates
Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Bylaw to assess property owners for Trounce Alley & Blood Alley Square maintenance
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would charge property owners in the Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square area for the 2025 maintenance costs of those public spaces. This is a 'local improvement' project — a way for the city to pay for upgrades or upkeep in a specific area by billing the properties that benefit from them. The bylaw would create a special tax or fee on those properties to cover the costs. The decision has not been made yet; Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own property in the Trounce Alley or Blood Alley Square area, watch for the council meeting date and consider attending to have your say.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 8.	A By-law to assess real property to defray 2025 maintenance costs for the Trounce Alley and Blood Alley Square Local Improvement Project
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw to assess parking costs on East Hastings
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would charge property owners along East Hastings Street to help pay for the 2025 costs of the East Hastings Street Collective Parking Project. A 'bylaw to assess real property' means the city would add a special fee to certain properties to cover the cost of a shared parking project. This is not yet approved — it is up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you own property on East Hastings Street.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body: 7.	A By-law to assess real property to defray 2025 costs for the East Hastings Street Collective Parking Project
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby housing agreement for 320-360 West 2nd Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a Housing Agreement for the property at 320-360 West 2nd Avenue. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and a property owner that locks in certain conditions — in this case, it would likely require that a portion of the new homes built there stay affordable or be rented at below-market rates for a set number of years. This is not yet approved; council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date — if you own or develop property in this area, this agreement could set a precedent for future affordable housing requirements.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 6.	A By-law to enact a Housing Agreement for 320-360 West 2nd Avenue
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Amendment to housing agreement for Prior Street, Malkin Avenue, Station Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend an existing Housing Agreement for properties at 450-496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue, and 1002 Station Street. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract that sets rules for how a building's units are used — often to keep them affordable or reserved for certain types of residents. This amendment would change the terms of that agreement. The decision has not been made yet; council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body: 5.	A By-law to authorize the amendment of a Housing Agreement Authorized by By-law No. 14507 (450 – 496 Prior Street, 550 Malkin Avenue and 1002 Station Street)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on zoning amendment at 1700-1750 West 75th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the zoning rules for the properties at 1700-1750 West 75th Avenue. This is an amendment to an existing Comprehensive Development (CD-1) bylaw, which is a special zoning plan for a specific area. If approved, the change could affect what can be built or done on these properties. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider and vote on the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you own or operate a business at this location.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
4.	A By-law to amend CD-1 (198) By-law No. 6254 (1700-1750 West 75th Avenue)
(Councillor Montague will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 1710-1730 East Pender Street to CD-1
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1710-1730 East Pender Street to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows for specific types of development that may not fit the standard zoning rules. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved yet. Councillors Maloney, Orr, and the Mayor must review the meeting proceedings before they are eligible to vote on this matter.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action is required from you at this time. Monitor the council meeting agenda for the vote date.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	A By-law to amend Zoning and Development By-law No. 3575 to rezone an area to CD-1 (1710-1730 East Pender Street)
(Councillor Maloney, Orr and Mayor Sim will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning at 6486 Chester Street to CD-1
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 6486 Chester Street to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows for specific types of development not normally permitted under standard zoning rules. This is a proposed change only — it has not been approved yet. Council will consider the bylaw at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this rezoning could affect nearby property values or development plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	A By-law to amend Zoning and Development By-law No. 3575 to rezone an area to CD-1 (6486 Chester Street)
(Councillors Bligh, Fry and Mayor Sim will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 1302–1318 East 12th Avenue to CD-1
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the properties at 1302–1318 East 12th Avenue to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone. A CD-1 zone is a custom zoning category that allows for a specific development plan, rather than following the standard rules for that area. This is a first step — the bylaw has not been approved yet, and council members named in the agenda must review the proceedings before they are eligible to vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own property or a business near this site, watch for the council decision — it could affect future development or land use in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	A By-law to amend Zoning and Development By-law No. 3575 to rezone an area to CD-1 (1302 - 1318 East 12th Avenue)
(Councillors Klassen, Maloney, Montague, Orr and Mayor Sim will need to review the proceedings to be eligible to vote)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning for high-density development at Cambie and Smithe
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for properties at 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street. If approved, this would change the land-use rules (called 'CD-1 Rezoning') to allow a new, larger development on these sites. The decision is not final — Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if you own property nearby or run a business in the area, attend or submit comments to have your say.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.	CD-1 Rezoning: 888-896 Cambie Street and 111-191 Smithe Street
Referral Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 Rezoning for 888 West 8th Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning for the property at 888 West 8th Avenue. The owner wants to change the zoning to a Comprehensive Development (CD-1) zone, which is a special type of zoning that allows for a specific development plan. This proposed bylaw would allow a new mixed-use building with commercial space on the ground floor and residential units above. Council will consider the referral report at an upcoming meeting before deciding whether to move forward with public hearings.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rezoning.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
2.	CD-1 Rezoning: 888 West 8th Avenue
Referral Report
agenda_briefs.article_title: Revised Regular Council Revised Agenda — April 14, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Fast-track rental housing and small hotels in new Burnaby mixed-use zone
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to create a new 'Mixed-Use Commercial District' that would fast-track approvals for rental housing and small hotels. If approved, this new zoning category would allow developers to build rental apartments and small hotels more quickly, without needing a full rezoning process for each project. The goal is to speed up the construction of much-needed rental homes and tourist accommodations. The proposal is still under review — it has not been passed yet. Council will consider the report at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments to have your say on this proposed zoning change.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	Fast-Tracking Rental Housing and Small Hotels – New Mixed-Use Commercial District
Referral Report
